problem: A function $f$ is defined recursively by $f(1)=f(2)=1$ and \[f(n)=f(n-1)-f(n-2)+n\]for all integers $n \geq 3$. What is $f(2018)$?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe what the function $f$ represents and how it is defined recursively?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Why is it helpful to define a new function $g(n)$ as $f(n) - n$? What does this new function represent?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: By substituting the recursive definition of $f(n)$ into $g(n)$, can you derive a simplified expression for $g(n)$ in terms of $g(n-1)$ and $g(n-2)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you find the initial values of $g(1)$ and $g(2)$? How do these values relate to the initial values of $f(1)$ and $f(2)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: By using the recursive relation for $g(n)$ and the initial values of $g(1)$ and $g(2)$, can you find a few more terms of the sequence $g(n)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Do you notice any patterns or recurring values in the sequence $g(n)$? If so, what is the period of this pattern?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can you determine the value of $g(2018)$ based on the periodicity of the sequence? Use this to find the value of $f(2018)$.
classified_questions.label: b
